name: vLLM
description: GPU-accelerated local LLM inference server — OpenAI-compatible endpoint backed by vLLM's continuous batching and PagedAttention. Recommended classroom engine for Maker Lab: the Phase 0 Ollama benchmark hit unusable p95 latencies at N=25 concurrent learners; vLLM is architecturally the right shape for that scale.
author: Crow

env_vars.name: VLLM_MODEL
env_vars.description: HuggingFace model ID to serve, e.g. 'Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct' or 'meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct'. Must fit in your GPU's VRAM (roughly: model params in billions * 2 GB for fp16, * 1 GB for int8).

env_vars.name: VLLM_HTTP_PORT
env_vars.description: Host port to expose the OpenAI-compatible endpoint on. Default 8089.

env_vars.name: VLLM_MAX_NUM_SEQS
env_vars.description: Max concurrent requests in flight (vLLM's continuous-batching slot count). 16 is a good classroom default; higher risks OOM.

env_vars.name: VLLM_GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION
env_vars.description: Fraction of GPU VRAM vLLM is allowed to claim. Lower it to leave room for other GPU services (SDXL, Whisper). Default 0.85.

env_vars.name: VLLM_HF_TOKEN
env_vars.description: HuggingFace token for gated models (Llama, Mistral instruct tunes). Leave blank for public models.

env_vars.name: VLLM_MODEL_CACHE_PATH
env_vars.description: Host path for the HuggingFace model cache (defaults to a named volume). Point at a drive with 40+ GB of free space if pulling larger models.

notes: Linux x86_64 + NVIDIA GPU required. Recommended classroom shape: Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct at VLLM_MAX_NUM_SEQS=16 on a 16 GB consumer GPU (tested in the Maker Lab Phase 0 benchmark, see bundles/maker-lab/PHASE-0-REPORT.md Spike 5). vLLM's OpenAI-compatible endpoint drops into Maker Lab's MAKER_LAB_LLM_ENDPOINT as-is.
